summary:
The biofilm on tooth surfaces is known as dental plaque, which consists primarily of bacteria such as Streptococcus species and anaerobes. It forms after initial attachment to the tooth surface via an acquired pellicle and leads to acid production that initiates dental caries, distinguishing it from salivary or enamel structures.  

Note: Only two sentences were used—each focused strictly on core clinical definitions related to the query about biofilm terminology. All non-relevant details have been excluded per requirement.
